title: Magnolia Oil & Gas Is in Lead to Acquire WildFire for Over $4 Billion
summary: Magnolia Oil & Gas Corp. has emerged as the front-runner to acquire closely held WildFire Energy for more than $4 billion in what would rank as its largest-ever acquisition, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: Dow Jones Finishes Day Down; Week Up | Closing Bell
summary: Comprehensive cross-platform coverage of the U.S. market close on Bloomberg Television, Bloomberg Radio, and YouTube with Romaine Bostick, Katie Greifeld, Carol Massar and Tim Stenovec. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Coffee Company Kahawa 1893 Works to Empower Women Farmers
summary: Margaret Nyambumo, founder of coffee company Kahawa 1893 and third-generation coffee farmer, said that she created the option to directly tip the female farmers who grow her coffee beans because she wanted to find a way to consistently and fairly compensate them regardless of fluctuation in coffee prices. Nyambumo said that the tips from customers — which are matched by the company — have been used by one of the farming collectives to start a scholarship for young girls to stay in school.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Education 'Getting Act Together' on Personal Finance: Cohen
summary: Abby Joseph Cohen, professor at Columbia Business School, joins Lisa Mateo and Tom Keene on "Bloomberg Money." They discuss personal finance and the US education system's effort to educate students on financial literacy. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Alternative Soda Brands Taking Over Beverage Markets
summary: Poppi Co-Founder Allison Ellsworth describes struggles of breaking into beverage industry, warns getting product on shelves is "capital intensive." Ellsworth says movement within "MAHA" is changing industry standards, including for beverage giants like Pepsi which purchased Poppi in 2025 for nearly $2 Billion. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Abby Joseph Cohen Talks Greenspan's Legacy
summary: Abby Joseph Cohen, professor at Columbia Business School, joins Lisa Mateo and Tom Keene on "Bloomberg Money." They discuss the legacy of former Fed Chair Alan Greenspan who died this week at the age of 100. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Look Ahead: US Jobs Report, America 250
summary: Lisa Mateo and Tom Keene discuss the week ahead for the markets and your money on this weeks edition of "Bloomberg Money." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Carl Richards on the Power of Compounding
summary: Barry sits down with Carl Richards author of "Your Money: Reimagining Wealth in Simple Sketches ." They discuss Carl's unlikely start in finance and building his own firm. Carl also breaks down how one sketch helped him translate wealth management and become a New York Times columnist.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Gold Climbs Above $4,000 as Inflation Print Curbs Rate-Hike Bets
summary: Gold extended gains above $4,000 an ounce on Friday after the latest US inflation print tempered expectations for an interest-rate hike, capping a tumultuous week that saw bullion slump to the lowest price since November.

title: キオクシア米上場計画、高市政権「370兆円投資」、投資のプロ4人が明かす1000万円投資術──1週間のニュース5選
summary: マーケットで話題になったニュースから、1週間を振り返る五つのトピックを厳選して紹介します。

title: Must-Read Books About Alan Greenspan's Legacy
summary:
Tom Keene and Lisa Mateo discuses the must-read books about Alan Greenspan's legacy on this weeks edition of "Bloomberg Money." Former Fed Chair Alan Greenspan died this week at the age of 100.
 (Source: Bloomberg)

title: US Oil Plunges Below $70 as Ships Keep Crossing Strait of Hormuz
summary: US oil plunged, erasing nearly all its gains made during the war, as vessel traffic through the Strait of Hormuz continued to rise and as investors increasingly expect the market to be oversupplied with Persian Gulf exports rebounding sharply.

title: EM Stocks Post Worst Week in More Than Three Months on AI Rout
summary: Emerging-market stocks posted their biggest weekly decline since early March, weighed down by a renewed selloff in technology shares that hammered South Korean equities.

title: Stocks Waver as Wall Street Rotation Gains Speed: Markets Wrap
summary: Wall Street traders kept driving a rotation out of high-profile chipmakers and into a broader category of companies tuned to improving growth prospects, with stocks wavering at the end of a volatile week.

title: AI Rout Exposes Wall Street’s $270 Billion Speculation Machine
summary: This week’s tech rout did more than puncture one of the year’s hottest trades: it exposed the machinery of modern speculation — and how quickly it can work in reverse.

title: US Chipmaker Selloff Pushes Key Stock Indexes to Weekly Loss
summary: US stocks ended the week lower as ongoing weakness in chip stocks outweighed a survey showing cooler-than-expected inflation expectations and easing rate hike concerns.

title: Bloomberg Money 6/26/2026
summary: Bloomberg Money takes the pulse of your financial life, powered by the reporting of our global newsroom. Today's guests are: Columbia Business School Professor Abby Joseph Cohen and Morgan Stanley Wealth Management Managing Director Daniel Skelly. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Hubble Pushes Satellite Network For Long-Range Bluetooth
summary: Alex Haro Co-Founder & CEO at Hubble Network joins Bloomberg Businessweek to the future of Bluetooth and the space economy.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Wall Street Embraces the Dollar as Warsh’s Fed Activates Bulls
summary: Only a year ago, Wall Street banks couldn’t come up with enough reasons to sell the dollar. Whether it was de-dollarization, “hedge America” or the debasement trade, the dollar was doomed.

title: Stock Valuations Should Worry Investors: Abby Joseph Cohen
summary: Abby Joseph Cohen, professor at Columbia Business School, joins Lisa Mateo and Tom Keene on "Bloomberg Money." Lofty stock prices may be hiding risks investors should be paying more attention to, especially in the labor market, according to Abby Joseph Cohen, the former Goldman Sachs partner and now Columbia Business School professor. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: TPG, Leonard Green Explore Sale of McIlroy-Backed Troon Golf
summary: The private equity owners of Troon Golf are exploring a potential sale of the company, which is one of the largest US golf course operators, according to people familiar with the matter.

title: CFTC Conducting Broad Probe Into Prediction Market Polymarket
summary: The US Commodity Futures Trading Commission is conducting an extensive investigation into Polymarket, according to a person familiar with the matter, the latest challenge for the prediction market platform plagued by operational stumbles and legal scrutiny.

title: Money Roundtable: Hidden Inflation, Social Security
summary:
Bloomberg's David Gura and Romaine Bostick join Lisa Mateo and Tom Keene on "Bloomberg Money." They discuss this week's US inflation report, Apple's price hike, and the future of social security.
 (Source: Bloomberg)

title: US Natural Gas Drops on Cooler Outlooks as July Contract Expires
summary: US natural gas futures settled much lower in the final day of trading for the July contract as midday forecasts shifted to show cooler weather compared with overnight outlooks. Individual transactions moved the market more amid a drop-off in liquidity near expiry.

title: South Korean Regulators Are Lamenting Leveraged ETFs
summary: Frenzied trading in South Korea has renewed concerns about the leveraged ETF boom

title: John Bolton Pleads Guilty to Mishandling Classified Material
summary: John Bolton, a former national security adviser to President Donald Trump who became one of his fiercest critics, appeared in court on Friday to plead guilty to unlawfully retaining national defense information.  Robert McWhirter, Constitutional lawyer and a practicing criminal defense and civil rights lawyer in Maricopa County, AZ  and Founder of the Law Office of Robert McWhirter, joined to discuss the case as well as the recent decisions from the Supreme Court.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Venezuela Quake Crisis Tests Legitimacy of Rodriguez Regime
summary: The earthquakes that struck Venezuela Wednesday have become the first major political test for acting President Delcy Rodríguez, with the disaster quickly evolving into a contest over competence. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Exxon’s Top US Gas Trader to Join Expand as Exits Hit Oil Giant
summary: Exxon Mobil Corp.’s head of US gas and power trading is moving to Expand Energy Corp., one of several recent departures from a unit the supermajor has been trying to grow.

title: Trump Says Iran Violated Ceasefire With Hormuz Drone Attack
summary: US President Donald Trump accused Iran of violating their ceasefire agreement by firing at cargo ships transiting the Strait of Hormuz, underscoring how unsettled the situation remains in a waterway critical to global energy flows. Rear Admiral Mark Montgomery (Ret.), former-Destroyer Squadron Commander in the United States Navy and Senior Fellow at the Foundation for Defense of Democracies joined Balance of Power to discuss.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Epic Fury Has Been an 'Epic Failure' Says Rep. Dean
summary: Congresswoman Madeleine Dean, a Pennsylvania Democrat and member of House committees on foreign affairs and appropriations joined Balance of Power to discuss the Iran war diplomacy and the administration’s budget requests. (Source: Bloomberg)
